## Summary

Reading Grade Level and Dialogue/Narrative Ratio now display **immediately** on the evaluation page — no longer gated behind evaluation completion. These are pure algorithmic computations (Flesch-Kincaid formula + quotation-mark detection) that run at submission time and store results in the job's `progress` JSONB.

Previously: both metrics showed "Not available" until the entire evaluation pipeline completed and produced an `evaluation_result_v2` artifact.

Now: metrics appear the instant the job is created — users see their manuscript's reading complexity and dialogue balance within seconds of submitting.

## Scope

- `app/api/evaluate/route.ts` — calls `computeEnrichment()` at job creation, stores `enrichment_reading_grade_level`, `enrichment_dialogue_percentage`, `enrichment_narrative_percentage` in `progress` JSONB
- `app/evaluate/[jobId]/page.tsx` — reads instant enrichment from `progress` as fallback when no evaluation artifact exists yet

Does NOT touch: evaluation pipeline, scoring, enrichment computation logic itself, or any other page.

## Branch Freshness (Never Behind)

Branch-Behind-Base: 0

## Risks & Anomalies

- Enrichment stored in `progress` JSONB (existing pattern used by `manuscript_word_count` sync). The pipeline's own `computeEnrichment()` call on completion will produce the authoritative values in the artifact — the page prefers artifact values when available.
- For existing-manuscript submissions (manuscript_id path), decodes the data-URI file_url to get text. If the URL format is non-standard or the fetch fails, enrichment gracefully skips (non-fatal try/catch).

---

No-Pipeline-Impact: Confirmed — no changes to evaluation scoring, LLM calls, or pipeline flow. Only adds pre-computation at the API submission layer and a display fallback on the page.
